meta.note: African zero-coverage sweep. Nearly all agent candidates already existed (Mongo Beti CM x2, Luuanda AO, The Rape of Sita MU). Only Honwana's PIDE-banned Mozambican collection was genuinely new. The remaining true zero-coverage African countries (Ghana, Zambia, Senegal, Guinea, Côte d'Ivoire, DR Congo, etc.) yielded no retraceable title-specific book ban — censorship there is documented at author/press/film level. Held: weak Satanic Verses possession-penalty mentions for Liberia/Sierra Leone.

entries.title: Nós Matámos o Cão Tinhoso!
entries.description_ban: Story collection ('We Killed Mangy-Dog') exposing racism under Portuguese colonialism; quickly banned and seized by the PIDE secret police shortly after publication, and the author, a FRELIMO militant, was imprisoned.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific colonial-era ban; documented via Wikipedia.
